summary: Matched the sysdolphin baselib MObj implementation through HSD_MObjLoadDesc, moving a large portion of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.s into src/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.c and adding MObj-related declarations. The PR also propagated newly identified symbol names across remaining assembly callers, including HSD_MObjReqAnim, HSD_MObjAnim, HSD_MObjCompileTev, HSD_MObjSetFlags, HSD_MObjClearFlags, hsdMObj, HSD_TObjLoadDesc, and hsdAllocMemPiece. The body notes a DObj parameter-ordering fix to match other files and that some matching data was prepared for MObj until static declaration requirements blocked further progress.
what_changed: Added 232 lines of C implementation in src/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.c and removed 487 lines from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.s, indicating multiple MObj routines were matched and migrated from assembly to C.
Added MObj declarations in src/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.h, with related declaration updates in DObj, PObj, and class headers.
Added mobj to e_files.mk and obj_files.mk so the newly matched C object participates in the build.

Renamed many assembly references from raw labels to identified MObj symbols, including func_80362E98 to HSD_MObjReqAnim, func_8036388C to HSD_MObjCompileTev, func_80362D38 to HSD_MObjSetFlags, and func_80362D50 to HSD_MObjClearFlags.
Renamed references to lbl_80405E28 as hsdMObj in callers that initialize or refer to MObj class metadata.
Renamed func_8035ED28 to HSD_TObjLoadDesc in at least one assembly callsite, reflecting adjacent baselib symbol identification needed by MObj loading work.
Renamed func_80381FA8 to hsdAllocMemPiece in class assembly and updated its local callsite.
Fixed DObj parameter ordering to match surrounding files, according to the PR body.
Prepared some matching data for later MObj work, but the PR body says progress stopped where static declarations became necessary.
smart_moves: Matched MObj incrementally only through HSD_MObjLoadDesc rather than forcing uncertain later routines, making the PR reviewable and preserving matching correctness.
Propagated newly known symbol names into existing assembly callsites across ft, gm, gr, mn, lb, it, text_2, and sysdolphin files, improving cross-file searchability and future decomp context.
Updated build files alongside the new C source so migrated code would be compiled as part of the normal object list.
Used header updates to expose MObj, DObj, PObj, and class relationships instead of relying only on local prototypes.
Explicitly noted the static-declaration blocker in the PR body, which gives future contributors a concrete stopping point rather than implying the module was fully resolved.
decomp_lessons: When a baselib subsystem starts matching, rename every known external caller in asm immediately; broad callsite renaming helps validate function identity and accelerates later module work.
Class-object work often requires global class metadata symbols such as hsdMObj to be named before constructor/load routines become readable.
Matching load-desc functions can expose dependencies on neighboring object systems; here MObj work touched DObj, TObj, PObj, class, and TExp-related declarations or callsites.
Parameter ordering in shared object helpers should be checked against related files, not only against the function currently being matched.
If static declarations are required to continue matching, record that boundary explicitly and avoid speculative conversion beyond the matched region.
Build integration is part of a successful asm-to-C migration: update object lists at the same time as adding the new source file.
naming_conventions: Public HAL/System Dolphin material-object functions use the HSD_MObj prefix, e.g. HSD_MObjReqAnim, HSD_MObjAnim, HSD_MObjCompileTev, HSD_MObjSetFlags, HSD_MObjClearFlags, and HSD_MObjLoadDesc.
Texture-object load helpers use HSD_TObj prefix naming, e.g. HSD_TObjLoadDesc.
Class metadata/global object identifiers use lower camel style with hsd prefix, e.g. hsdMObj.
Internal allocator naming uses hsd prefix lower camel style, e.g. hsdAllocMemPiece.
This PR replaces anonymous func_803xxxxx and lbl_804xxxxx names with subsystem-specific names when function identity is established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Large mobj.s deletion paired with new mobj.c source indicates direct asm-to-C migration while leaving unmatched assembly in place.
Renaming assembly call targets without changing instruction bytes keeps existing unmatched files link-compatible while documenting known APIs.
Class/global metadata references were identified by replacing lbl_80405E28 with hsdMObj at lis/addi address materialization sites.
The PR appears to preserve unmatched code as assembly stubs where matching was not yet possible due to static declaration constraints.

Callsite patterns helped identify MObj APIs: animation request calls followed by HSD_MObjAnim were renamed HSD_MObjReqAnim, and TEV compilation callsites over material objects were renamed HSD_MObjCompileTev.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body itself documents two implementation notes: DObj parameter ordering was fixed to match other files, and MObj matching data was prepared only until static declarations became necessary.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue MObj matching after HSD_MObjLoadDesc or resolve the static declaration blocker.
Search for remaining func_80362D38, func_80362D50, func_80362E98, and func_8036388C references to ensure all MObj callers were renamed.
Inspect subsequent changes to src/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.h for struct layout refinements introduced after this initial match.
Search for DObj parameter-ordering fixes in later PRs to see whether this convention was reused elsewhere.
Find later PRs involving hsdMObj class metadata or hsdInitClassInfo to understand the class-system naming pattern.
